title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. alaricum
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. alaricum, also known as a mountain hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium glaucinum plant, a perennial herb native to high-altitude regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The mountain hawkweed has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the aerial parts of Hieracium glaucinum subsp. alaricum are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from a range of health problems.
